$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the internal bridge REQUEST envelope shape (BridgeReq). Mirrors extension/src/shared/types.ts:BridgeReq / OpArgs; keep the two in sync (types.ts is not generated from this yet). This describes the CURRENT wide envelope: `op` is a plain string and `args` is a flat bag of optional fields shared across all ops. The typed / discriminated-union form (one args shape per op) is a planned follow-up (roadmap P1#3) and is NOT reflected here.
title: BridgeReq
description: A request from the MCP server, forwarded through the native host to the extension. Shape: { id, op, tabId?, args }.

id.description: Correlation id echoed back on the matching BridgeResp. Monotonic integer in practice; string is accepted for forward-compatibility.

op.description: The operation name (a tool's underlying op). Enumerated by contracts/tools.json; kept as a plain string here until the discriminated-union lands.

tabId.description: Optional target tab. When omitted, the handler resolves the active tab.

OpArgs.description: Arguments an op may carry. Every field is optional — each handler reads the ones it needs and validates them at runtime; the Rust side enforces the required ones per each tool's inputSchema in tools.json.
